name: Calvin Ridley
headline: Broken fibula and a shrinking role threaten Ridley's relevance
body:
The 2025 tape is alarming. Across just seven games before a fractured fibula ended his year, Ridley ran routes on only 24.6% of dropbacks, drew a 6.8% target share, and caught 17 of 36 looks for a 47.2% catch rate with zero touchdowns. That is a stunning collapse from his 2024 line, when he commanded a 23.7% target share, a 0.663 WOPR, and 1,017 yards over a full 17 games. A 12.58 air-yards-per-target figure confirms he was still being used downfield, but the volume and efficiency cratered.

Now 31 and reshuffled in Tennessee's pecking order behind rookie Carnell Tate downfield and Wan'Dale Robinson underneath, his path to consistent targets is narrow. Modest projections near 47-761-3 reflect that, and the ADP outside the top 70 receivers is fair. Treat him as a final-round dart with one realistic upside scenario: an in-season trade to a contender after injuries hit elsewhere.
